MoS2.reason: MoS2 exhibits characteristic E2g and A1g modes at ~383 and ~408 cm竅ｻﾂｹ respectively. The spectrum shows a broad feature at 300.8 cm竅ｻﾂｹ but lacks the distinctive doublet in the 380-410 cm竅ｻﾂｹ range.

WS2.reason: WS2 shows E2g at ~356 cm竅ｻﾂｹ and A1g at ~421 cm竅ｻﾂｹ. No peaks matching these positions are present.

Bulk graphite.reason: The 2D band at 2670.2 cm竅ｻﾂｹ has FWHM of 32.19 cm竅ｻﾂｹ, which is narrower than typical bulk graphite (>50 cm竅ｻﾂｹ), and the I(2D)/I(G) ratio of ~1.6 (4.41/2.70) is higher than graphite (~0.3-0.5).

Amorphous carbon.reason: The G band at 1579.9 cm竅ｻﾂｹ is sharp (FWHM 16.97 cm竅ｻﾂｹ) rather than broad. The D band at 1341.9 cm竅ｻﾂｹ has very low intensity (rel_height 0.11) compared to G, inconsistent with amorphous carbon which shows intense, broad D band.

key_evidence: Dominant peak at 519.9 cm竅ｻﾂｹ with rel_height 25.87 and FWHM 17.08 cm竅ｻﾂｹ matches crystalline silicon substrate (expected ~520 cm竅ｻﾂｹ)
Strong 2D band at 2670.2 cm竅ｻﾂｹ with FWHM 32.19 cm竅ｻﾂｹ - narrow width indicates few-layer graphene
G band at 1579.9 cm竅ｻﾂｹ with FWHM 16.97 cm竅ｻﾂｹ - sharp and consistent with spﾂｲ carbon
I(2D)/I(G) intensity ratio of 1.64 (4.41/2.70) suggests monolayer to few-layer graphene
Very weak D band at 1341.9 cm竅ｻﾂｹ with rel_height 0.11 indicates high crystalline quality with minimal defects
I(D)/I(G) ratio of ~0.04 confirms low defect density
Secondary Si peak at 971.8 cm竅ｻﾂｹ (rel_height 3.32) corresponds to second-order Si phonon mode
Region fractions show 49.7% intensity in 500-900 cm竅ｻﾂｹ (Si-dominated) and 8.4% in 2650-2750 cm竅ｻﾂｹ (2D band region)
Peak at 2450.8 cm竅ｻﾂｹ consistent with D+D'' combination mode in graphene
reasoning:
**Stage 1 - Dominant Features:** The spectrum is dominated by a massive peak at 519.9 cm竅ｻﾂｹ with relative height 25.87, which is by far the strongest feature. This matches the well-known first-order optical phonon of crystalline silicon at ~520 cm竅ｻﾂｹ. The narrow FWHM of 17.08 cm竅ｻﾂｹ confirms high-quality crystalline Si. The second strongest peak is at 2670.2 cm竅ｻﾂｹ (rel_height 4.41), followed by 971.8 cm竅ｻﾂｹ (rel_height 3.32) and 1579.9 cm竅ｻﾂｹ (rel_height 2.70). The 971.8 cm竅ｻﾂｹ peak is the second-order Si phonon mode.

**Stage 2 - Secondary Features:** The peaks at 1579.9 cm竅ｻﾂｹ and 2670.2 cm竅ｻﾂｹ are diagnostic of graphitic carbon. The G band at 1579.9 cm竅ｻﾂｹ (FWHM 16.97 cm竅ｻﾂｹ) arises from spﾂｲ carbon stretching. The 2D band at 2670.2 cm竅ｻﾂｹ is the double-resonance two-phonon mode. Critically, the D band at 1341.9 cm竅ｻﾂｹ is extremely weak (rel_height 0.11 vs G band 2.70), indicating very low defect density. The peak at 618.1 cm竅ｻﾂｹ may be a weak Si-related feature. A small peak at 2450.8 cm竅ｻﾂｹ corresponds to the D+D'' combination mode seen in graphene.

**Stage 3 - Spectral Weight Distribution:** The region_fractions confirm: 500-900 cm竅ｻﾂｹ contains 49.7% of intensity (dominated by Si), 2650-2750 cm竅ｻﾂｹ has 8.4% (2D band), 1550-1620 cm竅ｻﾂｹ has 3.7% (G band), and 1300-1400 cm竅ｻﾂｹ has only 0.4% (minimal D band). This distribution is consistent with graphene on Si where the substrate dominates but carbon signals are clearly present.

**Stage 4 - Candidate Evaluation:** The I(2D)/I(G) ratio of ~1.64 strongly suggests monolayer to few-layer graphene rather than bulk graphite (which shows ratio ~0.3-0.5). The 2D band FWHM of 32.19 cm竅ｻﾂｹ is narrower than bulk graphite and consistent with few-layer graphene. The extremely low D/G ratio (~0.04) indicates high crystalline quality. TMDs are excluded due to absence of their characteristic mode pairs. The 300.8 cm竅ｻﾂｹ feature is too broad and mispositioned for TMD assignment.

**Stage 5 - Decision:** The evidence unambiguously points to high-quality graphene (likely monolayer to few-layer based on 2D band characteristics) deposited on a crystalline silicon substrate. The Si peak dominance simply reflects the penetration depth and substrate contribution, while the carbon fingerprint is definitively graphene.
